title: Correctness, Completeness, Optimality, Metrics, and Selection · Language Clinic: Completeness Claim
summary: A solver wins from one timing, path length is compared across different mazes, and explored-cell animation is mistaken for useful work. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the completeness claim increment in solver benchmark and decision report. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: State the finite graph and resource assumptions under which a solver will find a path if one exists.
Validate endpoints, adjacency, passability, uniqueness policy, and reported cost against an independent route checker.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-solver-evidence-comparison-3
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-solver-evidence-comparison-3.
# Case 5798: a clean desktop release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: the player cannot rely on color or animation.
# Changed case: change the seed and retain replay identity.
def worked_maze_solver_evidence_comparison_3(fixtures: int = 24, valid_results: int = 24, solvers: int = 4, changed_cases: int = 8, isolated_metrics: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "gridworked3",
        "topology5798",
        "search3dekrow",
        "eventworked35798",
        "access5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if fixtures < 8 or valid_results < 0:
        return False, valid_results, "benchmark-fixtures"
    if valid_results != fixtures:
        return False, valid_results, "invalid-solver-result"
    if solvers < 4 or changed_cases < 4:
        return False, solvers, "solver-coverage"
    if not isolated_metrics:
        return False, changed_cases, "confounded-metrics"
    assert valid_results == fixtures
    return True, valid_results, "defensible-solver-comparison"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-completeness-claim-1m1xt6
# Competency: maze-completeness-claim.
# Case 2300: a clean desktop release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: all graph claims need an independent witness.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def evidence_maze_completeness_claim_1m1xt6(fixtures: int = 24, valid_results: int = 24, solvers: int = 4, changed_cases: int = 8, isolated_metrics: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1m1xt6 = "1m1xt6-6tx1m1-2300"
    assert evidence_variant_1m1xt6
    evidence_axes_1m1xt6 = (
        "grid1m1xt6",
        "topology2300",
        "search6tx1m1",
        "event1m1xt62300",
        "access23001m1xt6",
        "recovery6tx1m12300",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1m1xt6)) == 6
    if fixtures < 8 or valid_results < 0:
        return False, valid_results, "benchmark-fixtures"
    if valid_results != fixtures:
        return False, valid_results, "invalid-solver-result"
    if solvers < 4 or changed_cases < 4:
        return False, solvers, "solver-coverage"
    if not isolated_metrics:
        return False, changed_cases, "confounded-metrics"
    assert valid_results == fixtures
    return True, valid_results, "defensible-solver-comparison"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: State the finite graph and resource assumptions under which a solver will find a path if one exists.
